en: A:Can I interview you?
B: Yes, I would love to.
A:Can I interview you?
B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: So’o hila:an honasamez ay? ya:o ’am rima’ komita’ ’iSo’on kayzaeh ay?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?
B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: So’o rim’aen honasamez ay? ya:o ’iyakina somingozaw ’iSo’on noka SaySiyat ka kinbazae’an.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於賽夏族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legend of the Saisiat.

ab: rim’aen ya:o ’am rima’ ray ’os’oso’an, ’okik ray taew’an o.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: yako bazae’ So’o ra:am noka SaySiyat ka kaspengan, ’iyakina somingozaw ’iSo’on.
ch: A:我聽說您對賽夏族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I’ve heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Saisiat and would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: ma’an kinra:aman ’okik ’akoy, ’ana nakhini ma’an tiniSkobae:oh ma’ ’am Sae’te:.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: tasingozaw ’iSo’on ya:o kayzaeh ay ’omalingo’? nakhini kayzaeh mayhael hayza’ ka ’inalingo’ kakita’en.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.
B: Okay, no problem.
A: How old are you?

ab: yako monae’hea’ o maykaSpat tinal’oemaeh ila.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.
A: You’re so young.
B: No, I’m not! I’m old.
A: Where are you from?
B: I’m from the East River.

ch: A:請問您的太太也是賽夏族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a SaySiyat?
B:No, she’s a Atayal.
A: What languages do you speak?

ch: B: 我會說賽夏語和泰雅語。
en: B: I speak SaySiyat and Atayal.

ab: kaysa’an kinSiyae’ somingozaw ’iSo’on, payaka:i’ naehan nom ’omalep ka tiyawan kayzaeh ay?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: ma’alo’, niSo’ kaysa’an ka tinotoroe’ kayzaeh a tomal.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: ma’an pinanabih mina sa’owaz a tomal, noka tatini’ Sinbil ka pinayaka:i’.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: kaysa’an ka siningozaw papayhini, ma’alo’ a tomal niSo’ ka pinakra:am.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.
B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: yako ’am lobih ila, pakSiwa’ So: mowa:i’ naehan ’akoy ka baezae’en kinbazae’an.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.
B: Okay! Please take care.
